# Markdown → HTML Acceptance Render (Pandoc)

Preferred host toolchain for **human-facing acceptance surfaces** that start as
Markdown (milestone Plan acceptance pack first; other acceptance Markdown may
reuse the same script). Markdown remains the **source of truth**. HTML is a
**presentation layer** generated by local CLI tools — **not** by the model — so
conversion does **not** consume LLM tokens.

## Mandatory Load

Load when:

1. Granoflow **initialization / MCP first-run** offers host tools;
2. emitting or presenting a **milestone Plan acceptance pack** (or any later
   acceptance Markdown that opts into HTML preview);
3. rediscovering tools after the user says they installed them.

```text
granoflow_bundled_skill_reference(
  skillId: "granoflow-agent-workflow",
  referenceId: "markdown-html-acceptance-render"
)
```

## Necessity Class

| Class            | Value                                                                |
| ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `necessity`      | `preferred_method` (never `required_capability`)                     |
| Without tools    | Accept with **Markdown only** — do not block Planning closeout       |
| With tools ready | **Auto-convert** Markdown → self-contained HTML and present the HTML |

Declining install never blocks connection, Analysis, Plan Gate drafting, or
Markdown acceptance.

## Toolchain

| Component                                                         | Role                      | Required for HTML ready |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------- | ----------------------- |
| [Pandoc](https://pandoc.org/)                                     | MD → HTML, tables, images | yes                     |
| [mermaid-cli](https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid-cli) (`mmdc`) | Pre-render `mermaid`      | yes                     |
| Vendored `diagram.lua` (pandoc-ext/diagram)                       | Pandoc filter wiring      | yes (shipped in MCP)    |
| [PlantUML](https://plantuml.com/)                                 | `plantuml` UML blocks     | optional                |

Script (repo-relative):

```text
skills/granoflow-agent-workflow/scripts/render_markdown_acceptance_html.py
```

Vendored filter:

```text
skills/granoflow-agent-workflow/scripts/vendor/pandoc-ext-diagram/diagram.lua
```

## Init Consent (MCP / first-run)

During Granoflow initialization (after Local API connection; may be same turn
as the Product Builder skill pack, but **as a separate decision**):

1. Detect toolchain (`--probe` on the script).
2. If **ready**, record `markdown_html_toolchain.status: ready` and skip the
   install question.
3. If **not ready**, ask once for consent to install host tools. Plain-language
   must include:

   - HTML preview of Plan acceptance packs needs Pandoc + Mermaid CLI
     (PlantUML optional for UML);
   - conversion runs **locally and does not consume AI tokens**;
   - refusing is OK — acceptance stays Markdown-only.

4. Record consent in MCP-owned config (non-secret), preferred key:

```yaml
markdownHtmlToolchain:
  consent: unknown | offered | approved | declined
  lastProbeAt: <ISO-8601> | null
  lastProbeStatus: missing | ready | error
  missing: [pandoc, mmdc, ...] # when missing
```

Use `granoflow_setup_write_config` (dry-run → confirm → write) or an equivalent
host-visible note if config write is unavailable. Do **not** invent a second
secrets file.

5. Installation is **user/host-owned** (Homebrew, apt, npm, etc.). MCP does not
   silently install system packages. After the user confirms install completed,
   re-probe.

Ask-once at init for consent; a `declined` answer does **not** suppress later
acceptance-time suggestions (see below).

## Acceptance-Time Behavior (every pack presentation)

When generating or presenting a Plan acceptance pack (interactive **or**
unattended display-only):

1. Write / update the Markdown pack (SoT) as today.
2. Run `render_markdown_acceptance_html.py` on that `.md` (or `--probe` first).
3. Branch:

| Probe / render result  | Agent must                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ready` + HTML written | Apply **Plan Acceptance Preview Gate** below (clickable HTML is mandatory)                                                                                                        |
| `tools_missing`        | Present **Markdown** with a clickable `file://` (or host open) link to the `.md`; **every time** suggest installing Pandoc + Mermaid CLI (token-free); do not pretend HTML exists |
| `error`                | Present Markdown with clickable link; report the error; suggest re-probe                                                                                                          |

4. Pack frontmatter **Must** record:

```yaml
html_render:
  status: ready | tools_missing | error | skipped_markdown_only
  html_path: <path> | null
  html_file_url: <file://...> | null # required when status=ready
  markdown_path: <path>
  markdown_file_url: <file://...>
  link_emitted: true | false
  tool_probe: { pandoc: bool, mmdc: bool, diagram_lua: bool, plantuml: bool }
  note: <one line; include token-free reminder when suggesting install>
```

5. Interactive acceptance may be given after the user opens the HTML (when
   ready) or Markdown (fallback). The accepted artifact identity remains the
   **Markdown** path + content hash (HTML is derivative).

## Plan Acceptance Preview Gate (hard when HTML ready)

MCP does not render inside chat. The host **Must** expose a **clickable**
preview for the user — same bar as Prototype Preview Gate.

### Required user-visible block

Emit a short **Plan Acceptance Link** block (localize labels) that includes:

1. Milestone key / pack version / one-line what to review (copy, schema, flows,
   UML, test cases — only sections with `present: true`).
2. **Primary clickable link** when `html_render.status: ready`:
   - absolute `file://…/….html` Markdown link, **and**
   - host open when available (`open_resource` / IDE open / `open` on macOS) so
     one click opens the browser or editor preview.
3. Secondary clickable link to the Markdown SoT (`file://…/*.md`).
4. Explicit ask (interactive): accept / revise this pack after reviewing the
   opened page.

Example shape:

```markdown
## Plan 验收链接 · M2 v1

请打开 HTML 验收（表格 / 流程图已渲染；本地转换，不耗 token）：

- [打开 HTML 验收页](file:///…/milestone-plan-acceptance-M2-v1.html)
- [打开 Markdown 真值](file:///…/milestone-plan-acceptance-M2-v1.md)
  看完后回复「确认 M2 Plan」或指出要改的章节。
```

### Interactive

1. Emit the Plan Acceptance Link block **before** asking for pack acceptance.
2. Prefer opening the HTML via host tooling, then still leave the clickable
   links in chat for re-open.
3. **Stop** and wait for accept / revise (do not treat silence as accept).
4. If `html_render.status: ready` but the agent did not emit a clickable HTML
   `file://` (or host-equivalent open path) in the user-visible message, fail
   closed as `plan_acceptance_html_link_required` — do not claim the pack was
   presented for acceptance.
5. Claiming “HTML ready” while only pasting a bare path with no link markup /
   no host open also fails as `plan_acceptance_html_link_required`.

### Unattended

1. Still convert when tools are ready; emit the same clickable links as a
   **non-blocking notice** (does not consume interaction budget).
2. Append `{ milestone_key, version, html_file_url, markdown_file_url }` to the
   run's `plan_acceptance_link_ledger`.
3. At run close, emit a **Plan Acceptance Link Digest** listing every ledger
   entry. Omitting it when any HTML pack was authored fails closed as
   `plan_acceptance_link_digest_required`.
4. Missing tools: Markdown clickable link + install hint in the Residual
   Report; do not block the wave.

## User-Facing Install Hint (localize)

When suggesting install, include at least:

1. Why: better Plan acceptance preview (tables, images, Mermaid/UML).
2. Token: Markdown→HTML is a **local CLI** step — **no LLM tokens**.
3. Minimal commands (adjust per OS; do not claim MCP will run sudo):

```text
# Example (macOS / Homebrew) — user runs locally
brew install pandoc
npm install -g @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli
# optional UML:
brew install plantuml
```

Then: re-run the acceptance pack step (or ask the agent to re-probe).

## Fail-Closed / Soft Codes

| Code                                   | Severity                   | When                                                                                                            |
| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `plan_acceptance_html_link_required`   | **hard** (when HTML ready) | HTML file exists / `status: ready` but no clickable HTML link (or host open) was shown before asking acceptance |
| `plan_acceptance_link_digest_required` | **hard** (unattended)      | HTML packs authored in the run but closing Plan Acceptance Link Digest omitted                                  |
| `markdown_html_toolchain_missing`      | soft                       | Tools missing; Markdown acceptance continues (still require clickable `.md` link)                               |
| `markdown_html_render_failed`          | soft                       | Tools present but convert failed; Markdown acceptance continues                                                 |
| `markdown_html_render_skipped_so_t`    | n/a                        | Informational: HTML skipped by policy                                                                           |

Never fail closed Plan acceptance solely because HTML tools are unavailable.
**Do** fail closed when HTML **was** produced but the user was not given a
convenient clickable/open path.

## Must Not

- Replace Markdown SoT with HTML-only packs.
- Claim HTML was shown when only Markdown was presented.
- Ask for pack acceptance without the Plan Acceptance Link block.
- Spend model tokens “hand-writing” HTML that mirrors the pack when the script
  can run (or when tools are missing — then show Markdown, do not invent a
  second full HTML by LLM).
- Treat toolchain install consent as Analysis/Plan/Execution authorization.
